fault_hypothesis: On line 67, the overlap check uses `<=` and `>=` instead of `<` and `>`, causing adjacent (touching) windows to be incorrectly treated as non-overlapping. The correct condition should be `not (b < s or a > e)` to detect touching endpoints as overlaps.

spec.expr: all(not (b__1 < s or a__1 > e) == (not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e)) for s, e in timeline if b__1 != s and a__1 != e)

constraints.intent: The strict and non-strict overlap checks differ exactly when endpoints touch; the buggy line uses <= and >= which fails to detect touching-endpoint overlaps.

spec.expr: all((b__1 < s or a__1 > e) == (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e) for s, e in timeline if b__1 != s and a__1 != e)

constraints.intent: Before using b__1 in the overlap check, verify that strict vs non-strict comparisons only differ when endpoints are equal (touching), exposing the bug for touching cases.

spec.expr: not any(b__1 == s or a__1 == e for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: After the non-overlap branch (window accepted), no existing slot should touch the new window at either endpoint under the correct strict-comparison semantics.

spec.expr: not any(b__1 == s or a__1 == e for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: After result is computed (window accepted), verify that no touching-endpoint conflicts exist, which would be violated by the buggy <= / >= condition.
